problem.title: Reverse Linked List
problem.description: Given the head of a singly linked list, reverse the list, and return the reversed list.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Iterative Approach
solutions.description: Use three pointers to reverse the links iteratively.
solutions.code_python:
from typing import Optional

# class ListNode:
#     def __init__(self, val=0, next=None):
#         self.val = val
#         self.next = next

def reverseList(head: Optional[object]) -> Optional[object]:
    prev = None
    current = head
    while current:
        next_temp = current.next
        current.next = prev
        prev = current
        current = next_temp
    return prev

solutions.title: Solution 2 – Recursive Approach
solutions.description: Use recursion to reverse the list by reversing the rest and updating the head.
solutions.code_python:
from typing import Optional

def reverseList_recursive(head: Optional[object]) -> Optional[object]:
    if not head or not head.next:
        return head
    new_head = reverseList_recursive(head.next)
    head.next.next = head
    head.next = None
    return new_head

solutions.title: Solution 3 – Stack-based Approach
solutions.description: Use a stack to store nodes and rebuild the list in reverse order.
solutions.code_python:
from typing import Optional

def reverseList_stack(head: Optional[object]) -> Optional[object]:
    if not head:
        return None
    stack = []
    current = head
    while current:
        stack.append(current)
        current = current.next
    new_head = stack.pop()
    current = new_head
    while stack:
        current.next = stack.pop()
        current = current.next
    current.next = None
    return new_head

solutions.title: Solution 4 – Reverse in Groups
solutions.description: Reverse the list in groups of k (generalization of the problem).
solutions.code_python:
from typing import Optional

def reverseKGroup(head: Optional[object], k: int) -> Optional[object]:
    dummy = type('ListNode', (), {'val': 0, 'next': head})()
    prev_group_tail = dummy
    current = head

    while True:
        # Check if there are k nodes remaining
        check = current
        for _ in range(k):
            if not check:
                return dummy.next
            check = check.next

        # Reverse k nodes starting at current
        prev, node = None, current
        for _ in range(k):
            nxt = node.next
            node.next = prev
            prev = node
            node = nxt

        # Connect reversed group
        prev_group_tail.next = prev
        current.next = node
        prev_group_tail = current
        current = node
